fields.name: CMPM
fields.description: Compare match The CMPM bit is set by hardware to inform application that LPTIM_CNT register value reached the LPTIM_CMP register's value.

fields.name: ARRM
fields.description: Autoreload match ARRM is set by hardware to inform application that LPTIM_CNT register's value reached the LPTIM_ARR register's value. ARRM flag can be cleared by writing 1 to the ARRMCF bit in the LPTIM_ICR register.

fields.name: EXTTRIG
fields.description: External trigger edge event EXTTRIG is set by hardware to inform application that a valid edge on the selected external trigger input has occurred. If the trigger is ignored because the timer has already started, then this flag is not set. EXTTRIG flag can be cleared by writing 1 to the EXTTRIGCF bit in the LPTIM_ICR register.

fields.name: CMPOK
fields.description: Compare register update OK CMPOK is set by hardware to inform application that the APB bus write operation to the LPTIM_CMP register has been successfully completed.

fields.name: ARROK
fields.description: Autoreload register update OK ARROK is set by hardware to inform application that the APB bus write operation to the LPTIM_ARR register has been successfully completed. ARROK flag can be cleared by writing 1 to the ARROKCF bit in the LPTIM_ICR register.

fields.name: UP
fields.description: Counter direction change down to up In Encoder mode, UP bit is set by hardware to inform application that the counter direction has changed from down to up. UP flag can be cleared by writing 1 to the UPCF bit in the LPTIM_ICR register. Note: If the LPTIM does not support encoder mode feature, this bit is reserved. Please refer to .

enum_values.name: Set
enum_values.description: Counter direction change down to up

fields.name: DOWN
fields.description: Counter direction change up to down In Encoder mode, DOWN bit is set by hardware to inform application that the counter direction has changed from up to down. DOWN flag can be cleared by writing 1 to the DOWNCF bit in the LPTIM_ICR register. Note: If the LPTIM does not support encoder mode feature, this bit is reserved. Please refer to .

enum_values.name: Set
enum_values.description: Counter direction change up to down

fields.name: CMPMCF
fields.description: Compare match clear flag Writing 1 to this bit clears the CMP flag in the LPTIM_ISR register

fields.name: ARRMCF
fields.description: Autoreload match clear flag Writing 1 to this bit clears the ARRM flag in the LPTIM_ISR register

fields.name: EXTTRIGCF
fields.description: External trigger valid edge clear flag Writing 1 to this bit clears the EXTTRIG flag in the LPTIM_ISR register

enum_values.name: Clear
enum_values.description: External trigger valid edge Clear Flag

fields.name: CMPOKCF
fields.description: Compare register update OK clear flag Writing 1 to this bit clears the CMPOK flag in the LPTIM_ISR register

enum_values.name: Clear
enum_values.description: Compare register update OK Clear Flag

fields.name: ARROKCF
fields.description: Autoreload register update OK clear flag Writing 1 to this bit clears the ARROK flag in the LPTIM_ISR register

enum_values.name: Clear
enum_values.description: Autoreload register update OK Clear Flag

fields.name: UPCF
fields.description: Direction change to UP clear flag Writing 1 to this bit clear the UP flag in the LPTIM_ISR register. Note: If the LPTIM does not support encoder mode feature, this bit is reserved. Please refer to .

enum_values.name: Clear
enum_values.description: Direction change to up Clear Flag

fields.name: DOWNCF
fields.description: Direction change to down clear flag Writing 1 to this bit clear the DOWN flag in the LPTIM_ISR register. Note: If the LPTIM does not support encoder mode feature, this bit is reserved. Please refer to .

enum_values.name: Clear
enum_values.description: Direction change to down Clear Flag

fields.name: CMPMIE
fields.description: Compare match Interrupt Enable

fields.name: ARRMIE
fields.description: Autoreload match Interrupt Enable

fields.name: EXTTRIGIE
fields.description: External trigger valid edge Interrupt Enable

fields.name: CMPOKIE
fields.description: Compare register update OK Interrupt Enable

fields.name: ARROKIE
fields.description: Autoreload register update OK Interrupt Enable

fields.name: UPIE
fields.description: Direction change to UP Interrupt Enable Note: If the LPTIM does not support encoder mode feature, this bit is reserved. Please refer to .

fields.name: DOWNIE
fields.description: Direction change to down Interrupt Enable Note: If the LPTIM does not support encoder mode feature, this bit is reserved. Please refer to .

fields.name: CKSEL
fields.description: Clock selector The CKSEL bit selects which clock source the LPTIM will use:

enum_values.name: Internal
enum_values.description: LPTIM is clocked by internal clock source (APB clock or any of the embedded oscillators)

enum_values.name: External
enum_values.description: LPTIM is clocked by an external clock source through the LPTIM external Input1

fields.name: CKPOL
fields.description: Clock Polarity If LPTIM is clocked by an external clock source: When the LPTIM is clocked by an external clock source, CKPOL bits is used to configure the active edge or edges used by the counter: If the LPTIM is configured in Encoder mode (ENC bit is set), the encoder sub-mode 1 is active. If the LPTIM is configured in Encoder mode (ENC bit is set), the encoder sub-mode 2 is active. Refer to for more details about Encoder mode sub-modes.

enum_values.name: RisingEdge
enum_values.description: The rising edge is the active edge used for counting. If LPTIM is in encoder mode: Encoder sub-mode 1 is active.

enum_values.name: FallingEdge
enum_values.description: The falling edge is the active edge used for counting. If LPTIM is in encoder mode: Encoder sub-mode 2 is active.

enum_values.name: BothEdges
enum_values.description: Both edges are active edge. If LPTIM is in encoder mode: Encoder sub-mode 3 is active.

fields.name: CKFLT
fields.description: Configurable digital filter for external clock The CKFLT value sets the number of consecutive equal samples that should be detected when a level change occurs on an external clock signal before it is considered as a valid level transition. An internal clock source must be present to use this feature

enum_values.name: Immediate
enum_values.description: Any external clock signal level change is considered as a valid transition

enum_values.name: Clocks2
enum_values.description: External clock signal level change must be stable for at least 2 clock periods before it is considered as valid transition

enum_values.name: Clocks4
enum_values.description: External clock signal level change must be stable for at least 4 clock periods before it is considered as valid transition

enum_values.name: Clocks8
enum_values.description: External clock signal level change must be stable for at least 8 clock periods before it is considered as valid transition

fields.name: TRGFLT
fields.description: Configurable digital filter for trigger The TRGFLT value sets the number of consecutive equal samples that should be detected when a level change occurs on an internal trigger before it is considered as a valid level transition. An internal clock source must be present to use this feature

enum_values.name: Immediate
enum_values.description: Any trigger active level change is considered as a valid trigger

enum_values.name: Clocks2
enum_values.description: Trigger active level change must be stable for at least 2 clock periods before it is considered as valid trigger

enum_values.name: Clocks4
enum_values.description: Trigger active level change must be stable for at least 4 clock periods before it is considered as valid trigger

enum_values.name: Clocks8
enum_values.description: Trigger active level change must be stable for at least 8 clock periods before it is considered as valid trigger

fields.name: PRESC
fields.description: Clock prescaler The PRESC bits configure the prescaler division factor. It can be one among the following division factors:

fields.name: TRIGSEL
fields.description: Trigger selector The TRIGSEL bits select the trigger source that will serve as a trigger event for the LPTIM among the below 8 available sources: See for details.

fields.name: TRIGEN
fields.description: Trigger enable and polarity The TRIGEN bits controls whether the LPTIM counter is started by an external trigger or not. If the external trigger option is selected, three configurations are possible for the trigger active edge:

enum_values.name: SW
enum_values.description: Software trigger (counting start is initiated by software)

enum_values.name: RisingEdge
enum_values.description: Rising edge is the active edge

enum_values.name: FallingEdge
enum_values.description: Falling edge is the active edge

fields.name: TIMOUT
fields.description: Timeout enable The TIMOUT bit controls the Timeout feature

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: A trigger event arriving when the timer is already started will be ignored

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: A trigger event arriving when the timer is already started will reset and restart the counter

fields.name: WAVE
fields.description: Waveform shape The WAVE bit controls the output shape

enum_values.name: Inactive
enum_values.description: Deactivate Set-once mode, PWM / One Pulse waveform (depending on OPMODE bit)

fields.name: WAVPOL
fields.description: Waveform shape polarity The WAVEPOL bit controls the output polarity

enum_values.name: Positive
enum_values.description: The LPTIM output reflects the compare results between LPTIM_ARR and LPTIM_CMP registers

enum_values.name: Negative
enum_values.description: The LPTIM output reflects the inverse of the compare results between LPTIM_ARR and LPTIM_CMP registers

fields.name: PRELOAD
fields.description: Registers update mode The PRELOAD bit controls the LPTIM_ARR and the LPTIM_CMP registers update modality

enum_values.name: Immediate
enum_values.description: Registers are updated after each APB bus write access

enum_values.name: EndOfPeriod
enum_values.description: Registers are updated at the end of the current LPTIM period

fields.name: COUNTMODE
fields.description: counter mode enabled The COUNTMODE bit selects which clock source is used by the LPTIM to clock the counter:

enum_values.name: Internal
enum_values.description: The counter is incremented following each internal clock pulse

enum_values.name: External
enum_values.description: The counter is incremented following each valid clock pulse on the LPTIM external Input1

fields.name: ENC
fields.description: Encoder mode enable The ENC bit controls the Encoder mode Note: If the LPTIM does not support encoder mode feature, this bit is reserved. Please refer to .

fields.name: ENABLE
fields.description: LPTIM enable The ENABLE bit is set and cleared by software.

fields.name: SNGSTRT
fields.description: LPTIM start in Single mode This bit is set by software and cleared by hardware. In case of software start (TRIGEN[1:0] = '00'), setting this bit starts the LPTIM in single pulse mode. If the software start is disabled (TRIGEN[1:0] different than '00'), setting this bit starts the LPTIM in single pulse mode as soon as an external trigger is detected. If this bit is set when the LPTIM is in continuous counting mode, then the LPTIM will stop at the following match between LPTIM_ARR and LPTIM_CNT registers. This bit can only be set when the LPTIM is enabled. It will be automatically reset by hardware.

fields.name: CNTSTRT
fields.description: Timer start in Continuous mode This bit is set by software and cleared by hardware. In case of software start (TRIGEN[1:0] = '00'), setting this bit starts the LPTIM in Continuous mode. If the software start is disabled (TRIGEN[1:0] different than '00'), setting this bit starts the timer in Continuous mode as soon as an external trigger is detected. If this bit is set when a single pulse mode counting is ongoing, then the timer will not stop at the next match between the LPTIM_ARR and LPTIM_CNT registers and the LPTIM counter keeps counting in Continuous mode. This bit can be set only when the LPTIM is enabled. It will be automatically reset by hardware.

fields.name: COUNTRST
fields.description: Counter reset This bit is set by software and cleared by hardware. When set to '1' this bit will trigger a synchronous reset of the LPTIM_CNT counter register. Due to the synchronous nature of this reset, it only takes place after a synchronization delay of 3 LPTimer core clock cycles (LPTimer core clock may be different from APB clock). COUNTRST must never be set to '1' by software before it is already cleared to '0' by hardware. Software should consequently check that COUNTRST bit is already cleared to '0' before attempting to set it to '1'.

enum_values.name: Busy
enum_values.description: Reset in progress, do not write 1 to this field

enum_values.name: Reset
enum_values.description: Trigger synchronous reset of CNT (3 LPTimer core clock cycles)

fields.name: RSTARE
fields.description: Reset after read enable This bit is set and cleared by software. When RSTARE is set to '1', any read access to LPTIM_CNT register will asynchronously reset LPTIM_CNT register content.

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: CNT Register reads do not trigger reset

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: CNT Register reads trigger reset of LPTIM

fields.name: CMP
fields.description: Compare value

fields.name: ARR
fields.description: Auto reload value

fields.name: CNT
fields.description: Counter value

fields.name: IN1SEL
fields.description: LPTIM input 1 selection The IN1SEL bits control the LPTIM Input 1 multiplexer, which connects LPTIM Input 1 to one of the available inputs. For connection details refer to .

fields.name: IN2SEL
fields.description: LPTIM input 2 selection The IN2SEL bits control the LPTIM Input 2 multiplexer, which connect LPTIM Input 2 to one of the available inputs. For connection details refer to . Note: If the LPTIM does not support encoder mode feature, these bits are reserved. Please refer to .
